item.title: The Iran War Is Testing US Alliances and Alarming Farmers
item.description: The effects of the conflict are widespread.

item.title: India Plans 100 Airports, 200 Helipads to Boost Connectivity
item.description: India plans to build 100 new airports and 200 helipads in smaller towns and cities in coming years as the government seeks to boost regional connectivity, trade and tourism.

item.title: Iran War Shockwaves Threaten More Damage to US Allies
item.description: From Europe to East Asia, the high price of the war may soon get even higher.

item.title: Another Two India-Bound LPG Tankers Exit Gulf Through Hormuz
item.description: Two more India-bound tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas have exited the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, adding to the trickle of vessels making it through the narrow waterway.

item.title: Iran War Leaves Global Economic Leaders Searching for Answers
item.description: The Iran war is forcing soul-searching within the global economic elite on how to respond to a series of shocks that show no signs of abating.

item.title: Dip-Buyers Arrive to Pull Gold Back From Brink of a Bear Market
item.description: Opportunistic buyers are starting to emerge in the gold market after the biggest selloff in years, helping to keep bullion’s record-breaking three-year bull run intact.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Extensive Growth Toll From Iran War
item.description: Sentiment among the world’s service providers and manufacturers took a synchronized step down during the first month of the Iran war, with business surveys growing pessimistic about economic growth as oil prices surge.

item.title: Houthis Enter War as Iran Retaliates Over Nuclear Site Attacks
item.description: The Houthis launched ballistic missiles at Israel on Saturday morning, marking their entry into the monthlong Iran war that has already caused chaos in energy markets and killed thousands of people.

item.title: Buffett’s Zurich Acolyte Says Odds of Beating Market Keep Fading
item.description: Guy Spier keeps a bronze bust of the late Charlie Munger of Berkshire Hathaway Inc. in the hallway of his Zurich office. Yet the hedge fund manager, who has just closed up shop, says Buffett-and-Munger-style stockpicking doesn’t work any more.

item.title: Anthropic, the Pentagon, and the Future of Autonomous Weapons
item.description: How AI will reshape warfare.

item.title: Odd Lots: AI and the Future of Autonomous Weapons (Podcast)
item.description: The last big story right before the war in Iran started was the collapse in the relationship between the Pentagon and Anthropic, with the latter objecting to any potential use of its models in either fully autonomous weapons or domestic surveillance. Of course, this story immediately become more relevant with the start of the war, and the reporting that Anthropic’s technology was in fact utilized at the start of hostilities. But what does that mean? How are these models used? And what would a fu

item.title: Pakistan Gets Initial IMF Approval for $1.2 Billion of Loans
item.description: Pakistan reached an initial agreement with the International Monetary Fund to unlock about $1.2 billion from a $7 billion bailout program, as geopolitical risks from the conflict in the Middle East threaten to upend its economic recovery.

item.title: Goldman’s New Adviser Rishi Sunak Urges Small Firms to Adopt AI
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s bankers gathered at a library in Birmingham this week to guide small UK businesses on scaling up in artificial intelligence — and to warn them of the risks of ignoring the new technology.

item.title: Philippines Seeks 30-Day Price Cap on Rice as Fuel Costs Surge
item.description: A Philippine government council on price coordination has endorsed a plan to impose a 50-peso ($0.83) a kilo ceiling on imported rice as the war in Iran continues to drive up food and fuel costs.

item.title: Indonesia Starts First Southeast Asia Social Media Ban for Kids
item.description: Indonesia began enforcing restrictions on social media accounts for children under 16 on Saturday, becoming the first country in Southeast Asia to adopt nationwide curbs as governments worldwide take a tougher approach to limit harmful online content.

item.title: China Urges Private Grain Firms to Tap Whole‑Grain Policy Push
item.description: China is directing private grain processors to expand and upgrade whole‑grain production, part of a broader drive to strengthen food security and modernize its agricultural supply chain.

item.title: China Calls on US Firms to Tap Rural Revitalization Drive
item.description: US companies in China should seize opportunities from the Beijing’s rural revitalization drive and align their strengths with the needs of agricultural and rural development, Vice Agriculture Minister Zhang Zhili said.

item.title: Mongolian PM Quits to End ‘Political Deadlock’ in Parliament
item.description: Mongolia’s prime minister resigned on Friday to try to find a way out of the political deadlock that has gripped parliament.

item.title: Australia Resumes Some Operations at Cyclone-Hit Key Port
item.description: A major export port on Australia’s west coast handling iron ore, liquefied natural gas and other bulk commodities has reopened after cyclone damage forced a shutdown.

item.title: Australia Plans to Underwrite Fuel Purchases by Private Firms
item.description: Australia is set to introduce new powers to shore up fuel security, including measures to underwrite private sector purchases of fuel from the international market as a prolonged war in the Middle East heightens supply risks.

item.title: New $1 Billion Airport Near Delhi to Test India’s Buildout Boom
item.description: Realtors in Jewar are ecstatic. A construction frenzy sparked by an airport project has swept through this otherwise monotonous farming town near New Delhi.

item.title: Music Festivals Put Down Roots in Asia as War Makes Travel Tough
item.description: Global music festivals are moving closer to their audiences in Asia as war-driven travel disruptions reshape how far fans are willing to go.

item.title: Ex-Mozambique Finance Minister in ICE Custody After Travel Snafu
item.description: A former Mozambique finance minister convicted in the US for his role in a $2 billion bond fraud was set to be deported Thursday after his release from prison, but was instead detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, according to his lawyer.

item.title: Fund With Anthropic Stake Extends Drop in Stunning IPO Reversal
item.description: Shares of the Fundrise Innovation Fund fell sharply for a second straight day Friday, though remain well above the underlying value of its holdings in private tech firms including the potentially IPO-bound SpaceX and Anthropic PBC.

item.title: Argentina’s $150 Million Bond Sale Prices Risk After Milei’s Term
item.description: Argentina sold $150 million of a bond denominated in dollars on Friday that gauged investors appetite to finance the government beyond President Javier Milei’s first term.

item.title: Meta to Launch New AI Glasses Aimed at Prescription Wearers
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. plans to debut two new Ray-Ban smart glasses models next week intended for prescription wearers.

item.title: New York City Sues to Ban ‘Unlawful’ Empower Ridehail App
item.description: New York City sued ridehail app Empower and founder Joshua Sear, claiming the company has been operating unlawfully without a license the past few years even as the service gained popularity by offering cheap fares to customers.

item.title: Kailera Therapeutics Files for US IPO to Fund Obesity Drugs
item.description: Kailera Therapeutics Inc. filed for a US initial public offering to help fund the development of its pipeline of obesity drugs.

item.title: Musk Takes a Swipe at Bowtied Judge in Twitter Investor Case
item.description: Elon Musk took to his social media network to attack the judge in the trial he lost last week when a jury found the billionaire defrauded Twitter Inc. shareholders before acquiring the platform in 2022.

item.title: Your Questions on Apple’s Critical 2026, Answered by Mark Gurman
item.description: Bloomberg’s Apple expert, Mark Gurman, answered more than 50 questions from subscribers in a one-hour Live Q&A with Tim Stenovec. Read the full transcript.
